<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately.


<file name="packages/@n8n/agents/AGENTS.md">

<violation number="1" location="packages/@n8n/agents/AGENTS.md:102">
P3: EngineAgent example contradicts the documented contract that `build()` is protected; the example defines `build()` without an access modifier, making it public by default.</violation>
</file>

<file name=".github/workflows/util-backport-bundle.yml">

<violation number="1" location=".github/workflows/util-backport-bundle.yml:45">
P1: Hyphenated workflow_dispatch input is accessed with dot notation, which can break expression evaluation for manual runs.</violation>
</file>

<file name=".claude/skills/create-pr/SKILL.md">

<violation number="1" location=".claude/skills/create-pr/SKILL.md:169">
P2: New plan-embedding instructions can break or escape the fixed `EOF` heredoc used for `gh pr create --body`, making command parsing unsafe with raw plan content.</violation>
</file>

<file name=".github/workflows/release-publish-new-package.yml">

<violation number="1" location=".github/workflows/release-publish-new-package.yml:12">
P2: Hyphenated input `package-path` is accessed via dot notation (`github.event.inputs.package-path`), which is parsed as subtraction in GitHub Actions expressions. This will not resolve the input value; use bracket notation (`github.event.inputs['package-path']`) or rename the input.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/@n8n/agents/src/evals/parse-judge-response.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/@n8n/agents/src/evals/parse-judge-response.ts:26">
P2: Fallback pass/fail detection can misclassify plain text like `pass: false` as a pass because it only treats quoted JSON `"pass": false` and the word “fail” as failures.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/@n8n/agents/src/runtime/messages.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/@n8n/agents/src/runtime/messages.ts:92">
P2: Missing toolCallId values are coerced to an empty string, creating duplicate IDs and risking incorrect tool-call/result correlation.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/@n8n/agents/src/runtime/model-factory.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/@n8n/agents/src/runtime/model-factory.ts:15">
P2: `isLanguageModel` is too permissive; checking only for a `doGenerate` key can misclassify arbitrary objects and make `createModel` return invalid model instances.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="packages/@n8n/agents/src/runtime/model-factory.ts:42">
P2: Missing validation for non-empty model name allows malformed `provider/model` IDs (e.g. `openai` or `openai/`) to pass empty model names into provider SDK calls.</violation>
</file>
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>Architecture diagram</summary>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant App as Caller / SDK Client
    participant Agent as Agent (SDK Builder)
    participant RT as AgentRuntime (Core Loop)
    participant Mem as Memory Store (SQLite/Postgres)
    participant LLM as LLM Provider (Anthropic/OpenAI)
    participant Tool as Tool Handler

    Note over App,Tool: NEW: Agent Execution Flow (@n8n/agents)

    App->>Agent: generate(userInput, options)
    Agent->>Agent: ensureBuilt()
    Agent->>RT: initRun()
    
    RT->>Mem: Fetch History & Working Memory
    Mem-->>RT: messages + context
    
    loop Agentic Tool-Call Loop (Max 20 Iterations)
        RT->>RT: stripOrphanedToolMessages()
        RT->>LLM: generateText(messages + tools)
        LLM-->>RT: assistant message (text or tool-calls)
        
        alt assistant returns tool-calls
            loop For each Tool Call (Concurrency defined)
                RT->>Tool: execute(args)
                
                alt Tool requires Human-in-the-Loop
                    Tool-->>RT: NEW: SuspendedResult (branded)
                    RT->>Mem: saveCheckpoint(runId, state)
                    RT-->>App: NEW: return GenerateResult (finishReason: 'tool-calls', pendingSuspend)
                else Normal Execution
                    Tool-->>RT: result data
                end
            end
            RT->>RT: add tool-results to message list
        else assistant returns stop/finish
            Note right of RT: Exit Loop
        end
    end

    RT->>Mem: NEW: saveMessages(threadId, responseDelta)
    opt NEW: Working Memory Extraction
        RT->>RT: parse <working_memory> tags
        RT->>Mem: saveWorkingMemory(scope, content)
    end

    RT->>LLM: NEW: computeCost(usage, modelId)
    RT-->>App: GenerateResult (messages, usage, totalCost)

    Note over App,Tool: NEW: Resume Flow (After Approval)
    App->>Agent: approve(runId, data)
    Agent->>RT: resume('generate', data, {runId})
    RT->>Mem: loadCheckpoint(runId)
    Mem-->>RT: state
    RT->>RT: Insert resume data into tool-result
    Note over RT: Loop continues from Tool Execution step...
```
</details>

```mermaid
    participant Client

    Auth-->>Controller: allowedWorkflowIds[]

    Note over Controller,Repo: CHANGED: Combined auth check and data fetch
    Controller->>Repo: NEW: getTestRunSummaryByIdWithAuthorization(id, allowedWorkflowIds)
    
    Repo->>DB: findOne({ id }, relations: ['testCaseExecutions'])
    DB-->>Repo: testRun data

    alt Test run exists AND workflowId is in allowed list
        opt status === 'completed'
            Repo->>Repo: NEW: Calculate finalResult from testCaseExecutions
        end
        Repo-->>Controller: TestRunSummary
        Controller-->>Client: 200 OK (Data + finalResult)
    else Test run missing OR unauthorized workflow
        Repo-->>Controller: null
        Controller-->>Client: 404 Not Found
    end

    Note over Controller,DB: Optimization: Replaced two DB roundtrips with one query
```

**No issues found** across 2 files

<details>
<summary>Architecture diagram</summary>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Start as Start Command
    participant Settings as InstanceSettings
    participant License as License Service
    participant DB as Shared Database

    Note over Start, DB: Startup Flow: Multi-Main License Check

    Start->>License: isMultiMainLicensed()
    License-->>Start: false

    alt Instance is Leader
        Start->>Settings: isLeader
        Settings-->>Start: true
        Start-->>Start: Throw FeatureNotLicensedError (Fail Fast)
    else CHANGED: Instance is Follower (Retry Logic)
        Start->>Settings: isLeader
        Settings-->>Start: false
        
        loop Retries (Max 5 attempts)
            Start->>Start: Sleep (Exponential Backoff: 2s, 4s, 8s...)
            
            Start->>License: NEW: reload()
            License->>DB: Fetch latest license cert
            DB-->>License: Cert data (written by leader)
            
            Start->>License: isMultiMainLicensed()
            
            alt Success
                License-->>Start: true
                Note over Start: Continue Startup
            else Still Not Licensed
                License-->>Start: false
            end
        end

        opt All Retries Exhausted
            Start-->>Start: NEW: Throw FeatureNotLicensedError
        end
    end

    Note over Start, DB: Happy Path: Follower waits for Leader to initialize DB cert
```
</details>

body:
P2: Make `__prepareForTransfer` non-writable; expressions can overwrite it before the bridge calls it.
Prompt for AI agents ```text Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At packages/@n8n/expression-runtime/src/runtime/index.ts, line 62: Make `__prepareForTransfer` non-writable; expressions can overwrite it before the bridge calls it. @@ -53,6 +59,7 @@ globalThis.SafeError = SafeError; globalThis.createDeepLazyProxy = createDeepLazyProxy; globalThis.resetDataProxies = resetDataProxies; +globalThis.__prepareForTransfer = __prepareForTransfer; // Initialize empty __data object (populated by resetDataProxies before each evaluation) ```
```suggestion Object.defineProperty(globalThis, '__prepareForTransfer', { value: __prepareForTransfer, writable: false, configurable: false, }); ```
author: cubic-dev-ai

body:
P2: Recurse into `Map`/`Set` contents before the non-plain-object early return, otherwise nested Luxon values still lose their type across the boundary.
Prompt for AI agents ```text Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At packages/@n8n/expression-runtime/src/runtime/serialize.ts, line 35: Recurse into `Map`/`Set` contents before the non-plain-object early return, otherwise nested Luxon values still lose their type across the boundary. @@ -0,0 +1,43 @@ + // Only walk plain objects — structured-cloneable types like Date, Map, Set, + // RegExp, Error, typed arrays survive copy:true with prototypes intact. + const proto = Object.getPrototypeOf(value); + if (proto !== Object.prototype && proto !== null) return value; + + // Plain object — walk values ```
author: cubic-dev-ai

body: Valid concern, but this applies to all runtime globals (`DateTime`, `SafeObject`, `resetDataProxies`, `createDeepLazyProxy`, etc.) — they're all assigned the same way. Fixing just this one would be inconsistent. Created [CAT-2614](https://linear.app/n8n/issue/CAT-2614) to address all of them together.

body:
The test is being rewritten to still assert that global built-ins from the allow-list can be used, while still making sure that it passes with both engines. 

Whereas the old engine will actually return an interval or a duration and the new engine will return a string, which makes more sense, because this is also what it will be serialized into when stored in the database, and also returning an interval or a duration is forbidden by certain nodes like the set node — it will actually throw because it does type validation after an expression.

body:
**19 issues found** across 31 files

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately.


<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/test/vector-search.workflow.json">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/test/vector-search.workflow.json:79">
P2: The query fixture asks for a `content` column that this workflow never syncs into the index. Use the same column name as the created index (`text`), otherwise this example cannot round-trip against a real Databricks index.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts:63">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Cross-credential data leakage: module-level caches are keyed only by `host`, not by credential identity. In a multi-user n8n instance, different users with different Databricks permissions on the same host will share cached dropdown results, exposing resources (catalogs, schemas, tables, volumes, functions, endpoints, warehouses) across credential boundaries.

Include a credential-specific identifier (e.g., the credential ID from `this.getCredentials()`) in the cache key so each credential gets its own cache slot.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts:1158">
P2: Missing trailing-slash strip on `host` for the `uploadFile` operation. Every other operation calls `.replace(/\/$/, '')` on `credentials.host`, but this one uses it raw, risking a double-slash in the URL.</violation>

<violation number="3" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts:1172">
P2: Missing `pairedItem` on the `uploadFile` return. Every other file operation includes `pairedItem: { item: i }`.</violation>

<violation number="4" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts:1513">
P2: Missing `pairedItem` on the Genie operations return. All other code paths include `pairedItem: { item: i }` for correct item linking.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/files/parameters.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/files/parameters.ts:50">
P2: `listDirectory` should not require `directoryPath`; as written, users can't list the root of a volume even though the API supports it.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/test/databricks-sql.workflow.json">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/test/databricks-sql.workflow.json:14">
P2: Set `resource` and `operation` explicitly in this workflow fixture instead of relying on the node defaults.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/unityCatalog.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/unityCatalog.ts:1">
P2: This file duplicates the existing Unity Catalog resource definitions, but it is not exported anywhere, so changes here will never affect the Databricks node and will drift from the real implementation.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/modelServing/parameters.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/modelServing/parameters.ts:3">
P3: This adds a second `modelServingParameters` definition while the old one still exists, leaving two divergent sources of truth for the model-serving schema.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/credentials/DatabricksOAuth2Api.credentials.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/credentials/DatabricksOAuth2Api.credentials.ts:60">
P1: The credential test is tied to Model Serving, so valid Databricks OAuth credentials will be rejected on workspaces that can use the rest of this node but lack serving-endpoint access. According to linked Linear issue NODE-4282, this credential is meant to support several non-serving Databricks APIs as well, so the test should use a neutral workspace endpoint instead.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/files.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/files.ts:1">
P2: This new file is dead duplicate code: the Databricks node imports the split `files/operations` and `files/parameters` modules, so edits here never affect runtime behavior.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/credentials/DatabricksApi.credentials.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/credentials/DatabricksApi.credentials.ts:49">
P1: Use a workspace-generic credential test instead of the Model Serving API. As written, valid Databricks credentials without Model Serving access can be rejected at save time.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/vectorSearch/parameters.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/vectorSearch/parameters.ts:191">
P1: Default this to `ANN`. Leaving `HYBRID` as the default makes vector queries invalid because `query_text` is hidden when `queryType` is `vector`.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/unityCatalog/parameters.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/unityCatalog/parameters.ts:257">
P2: `storage_location` needs to be enforced when `volumeType` is `EXTERNAL`; otherwise the node allows invalid external-volume requests that Databricks rejects.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/databricksSql/parameters.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/databricksSql/parameters.ts:138">
P3: Remove the unreachable `queryId` parameter; this node never exposes update/delete query operations.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/test/Databricks.node.test.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/test/Databricks.node.test.ts:14">
P2: This test only covers the immediate `SUCCEEDED` SQL response, so it won't catch regressions in the new polling or chunk-fetch logic.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/test/Databricks.node.test.ts:143">
P2: This mock disables OpenAPI-based schema detection, so the test never covers the request-format detection and validation logic that the new model-serving node adds.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/databricksSql.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/databricksSql.ts:1">
P2: This file is dead duplicate code: the node exports SQL resources from `resources/databricksSql/{operations,parameters}.ts`, so these new definitions are never used.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/modelServing.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/modelServing.ts:3">
P1: This new model-serving config is not wired into the node, so the added operations and parameters never take effect.</violation>
</file>
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>Architecture diagram</summary>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant WF as n8n Workflow Engine
    participant Node as Databricks Node
    participant Cred as Credentials (PAT/OAuth2)
    participant D_API as Databricks REST API
    participant SQL as SQL Warehouse/Genie

    Note over WF, D_API: Authentication Layer
    Node->>Cred: NEW: Get Host & Auth Type
    alt OAuth2
        Cred->>D_API: POST /oidc/v1/token
        D_API-->>Cred: Access Token
    else Personal Access Token
        Cred-->>Node: Static Bearer Token
    end

    Note over WF, D_API: Resource: Model Serving (Auto-Detection Flow)
    Node->>D_API: NEW: GET /api/2.0/serving-endpoints/{name}/openapi
    D_API-->>Node: OpenAPI Schema
    Node->>Node: NEW: detectInputFormat() (Chat, Completions, etc.)
    Node->>Node: NEW: validateRequestBody() against schema
    Node->>D_API: NEW: POST /serving-endpoints/{name}/invocations

    Note over WF, D_API: Resource: SQL & Genie (Async Polling Flow)
    Node->>D_API: NEW: POST /api/2.0/sql/statements (with wait_timeout)
    D_API-->>Node: status: PENDING / RUNNING + statement_id

    loop Status Polling
        Node->>D_API: NEW: GET /api/2.0/sql/statements/{statement_id}
        D_API-->>Node: status: SUCCEEDED / FAILED
    end

    alt SUCCEEDED
        Node->>D_API: NEW: GET /api/2.0/sql/statements/{id}/result/chunks/{n}
        D_API-->>Node: Chunked Result Set
        Node->>WF: Return rows as separate n8n items
    else FAILED
        Node-->>WF: Throw NodeOperationError
    end

    Note over WF, D_API: Resource: Files (Binary Data Flow)
    WF->>Node: Input with Binary Property
    Node->>Node: CHANGED: Extract Buffer from dataFieldName
    alt Upload File
        Node->>D_API: NEW: PUT /api/2.0/fs/files/{path} (Up to 5GiB)
    else Download File
        D_API-->>Node: Binary Stream
        Node->>WF: Create n8n Binary Object
    end
```
</details>

body:
<!-- metadata:{"confidence":9} -->
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Cross-credential data leakage: module-level caches are keyed only by `host`, not by credential identity. In a multi-user n8n instance, different users with different Databricks permissions on the same host will share cached dropdown results, exposing resources (catalogs, schemas, tables, volumes, functions, endpoints, warehouses) across credential boundaries.

Include a credential-specific identifier (e.g., the credential ID from `this.getCredentials()`) in the cache key so each credential gets its own cache slot.

<details>

body:
I've fixed the test so the "Model Serving -> Query Endpoint" test now exercises the OpenAPI-based schema detection and request validation logic. Two changes were made:

1. **`Databricks.node.test.ts`** (line ~208): Replaced the empty-array OpenAPI mock (`[]`) with a realistic chat-format OpenAPI schema containing `servers`, `paths`, `requestBody` with a `messages` property in a `oneOf` schema. This ensures `detectInputFormat()` runs and identifies the `'chat'` format, and `validateRequestBody()` validates the request body against it.

2. **`model-serving.workflow.json`**: Changed `requestBody` from a JSON string (`"{\"messages\":...}"`) to a parsed object (`{ "messages": [...] }`) so that `validateRequestBody` can properly check the `messages` array field. Updated expected `pinData` from `"detectedFormat": "generic"` to `"detectedFormat": "chat"` to match the detected schema.

✅ All 9 Databricks tests passing
✅ Typecheck passes
✅ Lint passes

PR: https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/27168

body:
**16 issues found** across 29 files

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately.


<file name="packages/nodes-base/package.json">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/package.json:89">
P1: These credentials validate against `/api/2.0/serving-endpoints`, which is tied to Model Serving rather than a workspace-generic check. Users without Model Serving access can be blocked from saving credentials even when they only need SQL, Files, or Unity Catalog.

(Based on your team's feedback about Databricks credential tests.) [FEEDBACK_USED]</violation>

<violation number="2" location="packages/nodes-base/package.json:496">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Do not send authenticated model-serving requests to `servers[0].url` from the endpoint's OpenAPI schema. This newly registered node will forward the Databricks bearer token to any absolute URL returned there, enabling credential exfiltration.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts:1314">
P2: Missing trailing-slash stripping on `credentials.host` in the `genie` handler. Every other resource handler uses `credentials.host.replace(/\/$/, '')`, but here the raw `host` is used, risking double-slash URLs.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts:1491">
P1: Custom agent: **Quality & Performance Review**

`push.apply` will throw `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded` when `data_array` contains more rows than the engine's argument-count limit (~100K). Use `for…of` or `Array.prototype.concat` to safely append large chunks.</violation>

<violation number="3" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts:1511">
P1: Custom agent: **Quality & Performance Review**

Same `push.apply` stack-overflow risk as above when fetching remaining result chunks.</violation>

<violation number="4" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/Databricks.node.ts:1561">
P1: The OpenAPI endpoint returns a single object, not an array. Casting the response as `OpenAPISchema[]` and checking `.length > 0` means schema detection is always silently skipped — the condition is never true because `.length` on a plain object is `undefined`.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/unityCatalog/operations.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/unityCatalog/operations.ts:13">
P2: According to linked Linear issue NODE-4282, Unity Catalog tables should support CRUD here, but this operation list only exposes get/list for tables, so create/update/delete table actions cannot be used.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/files/operations.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/files/operations.ts:29">
P3: These descriptions are misleading: this resource operates on Unity Catalog volumes, not workspace files. According to linked Linear issue NODE-4282, the file scope here is volume-based, so the current labels set the wrong expectation for delete/download/metadata actions.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/files/parameters.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/files/parameters.ts:56">
P2: `listDirectory` should not reuse the required `Directory Path` field, because that prevents listing the root of a volume.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/credentials/DatabricksApi.credentials.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/credentials/DatabricksApi.credentials.ts:49">
P2: Use a workspace-generic credential test endpoint instead of `serving-endpoints`. Valid Databricks credentials can fail Save/Test here when the workspace does not expose Model Serving.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/credentials/DatabricksOAuth2Api.credentials.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/credentials/DatabricksOAuth2Api.credentials.ts:60">
P2: Use a workspace-generic endpoint for credential validation instead of the Model Serving API. Valid Databricks OAuth credentials will be rejected on workspaces that do not expose or permit serving endpoints.

(Based on your team's feedback about avoiding Databricks Public Preview API endpoints for credential tests.) [FEEDBACK_USED]</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/unityCatalog/parameters.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/unityCatalog/parameters.ts:36">
P2: The manual-name validator is too strict for Unity Catalog object names and rejects valid backtick-escaped or non-ASCII identifiers.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/vectorSearch/parameters.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/resources/vectorSearch/parameters.ts:76">
P2: `queryVector` is exposed as JSON, but the vector-query execution path forwards it without parsing, so Databricks receives a string instead of a number array.</violation>

<file name="packages/nodes-base/nodes/Databricks/test/model-serving-chat.workflow.json">
```

<details>
<summary>Architecture diagram</summary>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Engine as n8n Workflow Engine
    participant Node as Databricks Node
    participant Auth as Credential Store
    participant API as Databricks API

    Note over Engine, API: NEW: Databricks Node Integration Flow

    Engine->>Node: Execute Node (Resource/Operation)
    Node->>Auth: NEW: Get Auth (PAT or OAuth2)
    Auth-->>Node: Bearer Token + Host

    alt NEW: Databricks SQL (Async Polling Flow)
        Node->>API: POST /api/2.0/sql/statements (query + warehouse_id)
        API-->>Node: 200 OK (statement_id, status: PENDING/RUNNING)
        
        loop Until Status is SUCCEEDED, FAILED, or CANCELED
            Node->>Node: sleep() based on polling logic
            Node->>API: GET /api/2.0/sql/statements/{statement_id}
            API-->>Node: Updated status + manifest (if finished)
        end
        
        Note right of Node: Maps data_array rows to individual n8n items
        Node-->>Engine: Array of items
    
    else NEW: Model Serving (Auto-detection Flow)
        Node->>API: GET /api/2.0/serving-endpoints/{name}/openapi
        API-->>Node: OpenAPI Schema JSON
        
        Node->>Node: detectInputFormat(schema) -> (chat, completions, embeddings, etc.)
        Node->>Node: validateRequestBody() vs detected schema
        
        alt Valid Body
            Node->>API: POST {invocationUrl} (from schema)
            API-->>Node: Model Prediction Response
            Node-->>Engine: Result + _metadata (detectedFormat)
        else Invalid Body
            Node-->>Engine: Throw NodeOperationError (with example payload)
        end

    else NEW: Files (Unity Catalog Volumes)
        alt Operation: uploadFile
            Engine->>Node: Binary Data (Buffer)
            Node->>API: PUT /api/2.0/fs/files/{path} (Overwrite flag supported)
            API-->>Node: 200 OK
        else Operation: downloadFile
            Node->>API: GET /api/2.0/fs/files/{path}
            API-->>Node: Binary Stream
            Node-->>Engine: n8n Binary Object
        end

    else NEW: Genie (AI Data Assistant)
        Node->>API: POST /api/2.0/genie/spaces/{id}/start-conversation
        API-->>Node: conversation_id + message_id
        Note over Node, API: Polling occurs if status is not COMPLETED
        Node-->>Engine: AI Response + SQL Attachments
    end
```
</details>

body:
The issue is not valid. The chat format detection test proves it: pinData expects "detectedFormat": "chat", which can only be set if openApiResponse?.length > 0 evaluates to true and detectInputFormat(openApiResponse[0]) runs. That test passes, confirming the real API does return an array and the code handles it correctly.

body:

P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Missing fallback in access-control conditional allows unscoped execution queries. If neither `user && sharingOptions` nor `accessibleWorkflowIds` is provided, no `WHERE` clause restricts results, potentially returning all executions to any caller. Add a defensive `else` branch that either throws or applies `qb.where('1 = 0')` to deny access by default.

<details>

If neither `user && sharingOptions` nor `accessibleWorkflowIds` is provided, no `WHERE` clause restricts results, potentially returning all executions to any caller. Add a defensive `else` branch that either throws or applies `qb.where('1 = 0')` to deny access by default.</comment>

```text
<file context>
@@ -981,8 +985,21 @@ export class ExecutionRepository extends Repository<ExecutionEntity> {
+			subquery.andWhere('"sw"."workflowId" = execution."workflowId"');
+			qb.where(`EXISTS (${subquery.getQuery()})`);
+			qb.setParameters(subquery.getParameters());
+		} else if (accessibleWorkflowIds) {
+			// Array-based access control (backward compat)
+			qb.where('execution.workflowId IN (:...accessibleWorkflowIds)', { accessibleWorkflowIds });
</file context>
```

</details>

```suggestion
		} else if (accessibleWorkflowIds) {

			qb.where('execution.workflowId IN (:...accessibleWorkflowIds)', { accessibleWorkflowIds });
		} else {
			// No access control provided — deny all to prevent unscoped queries
			qb.where('1 = 0');
```

body:
## Pull request overview Adds first-class support for exporting Google Docs to Markdown when using the Google Drive node’s **File → Download** operation, aligning the node UI with Drive’s `text/markdown` export capability. **Changes:** - Exposes `Markdown (md)` / `text/markdown` in the Google Docs export format dropdown for the download operation - Adds a unit test asserting `mimeType: text/markdown` is sent to the Drive export endpoint - Adds a workflow test covering the Google Docs → Markdown download flow ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | packages/nodes-base/nodes/Google/Drive/v2/actions/file/download.operation.ts | Adds `Markdown (md)` (`text/markdown`) to Google Docs export options. | | packages/nodes-base/nodes/Google/Drive/test/v2/node/file/download.test.ts | Adds unit test for Markdown export and resets mocks between tests. | | packages/nodes-base/nodes/Google/Drive/v2/test/GoogleDriveV2.workflow.test.ts | Adds nock mocks + includes new workflow test file in the harness. | | packages/nodes-base/nodes/Google/Drive/v2/test/file-download-docs-markdown.workflow.json | New workflow fixture to validate Docs → Markdown download configuration. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

You can also share your feedback on Copilot code review. [Take the survey](https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/XP6L3XJ).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This download unit test sets `nodeParameters.operation` to `deleteFile` (and includes delete-specific options), which is unrelated to the download operation being exercised. This looks like a copy/paste from the delete test and makes the test misleading. Update the parameters to represent a download (e.g. `operation: 'download'`) and remove delete-only options so the test matches the behavior under test.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The new Markdown export test omits `operation` in `nodeParameters`, while other GoogleDriveV2 node tests typically include it. Adding `operation: 'download'` (and any other required top-level params used by the real node) would make this test more representative and less likely to become invalid if parameter access changes in the execute implementation.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

<details>
<summary>Architecture diagram</summary>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User / UI
    participant Node as Google Drive Node
    participant Transport as n8n Transport Layer
    participant GDrive as Google Drive API

    Note over User,GDrive: Google Doc Download with Export Conversion

    User->>Node: Execute Download (File ID, Option: Markdown)
    
    Node->>Transport: request('GET', '/drive/v3/files/{fileId}')
    Transport->>GDrive: Fetch Metadata (fields: mimeType, name)
    GDrive-->>Transport: 200 OK (mimeType: application/vnd.google-apps.document)
    Transport-->>Node: Metadata

    alt Is Google Doc (requires export)
        Note right of Node: CHANGED: docsToFormat set to "text/markdown"
        Node->>Transport: request('GET', '/drive/v3/files/{fileId}/export')
        Transport->>GDrive: GET /export?mimeType=text/markdown
        
        alt Success
            GDrive-->>Transport: 200 OK (Binary Data, Content-Type: text/markdown)
            Transport-->>Node: ArrayBuffer / Stream
            Node-->>User: Output Binary File (.md)
        else Error (Unsupported Format)
            GDrive-->>Transport: 400/403 Error
            Transport-->>Node: Error Response
            Node-->>User: Workflow Error
        end
    else Standard File
        Node->>Transport: request('GET', '/drive/v3/files/{fileId}?alt=media')
        Transport->>GDrive: Download direct binary
        GDrive-->>Node: Binary Data
        Node-->>User: Output Binary File
    end
```
</details>

author: guillaumejacquart

body: Thanks for the update—glad it’s handled in the validateOption error catching.
author: cubic-dev-ai

body: This is intentional. Network error wrapping is scoped to `validateOptions()` only (config-time), not `fetchMetadata()` (shared path). At runtime, a transient network outage after cache expiry should surface as a 500 (server issue), not a 400 (invalid config). The `validateOptions` method already wraps the `fetchMetadata` call to catch network errors and convert them to `IdentifierValidationError`.
author: guillaumejacquart

body: Same as above — this is intentional. Network error wrapping lives in `validateOptions()` (config-time only), not in `fetchMetadata()` which is shared with `resolve()`. Transient network failures at runtime should be 500s, not 400 validation errors.

body:
**4 issues found** across 11 files
Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues) ```text Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately. P1: Avoid tagging the no-op fallback in `apiKeyHasScope()`. Otherwise `/discover` can hide endpoints that are still accessible when API-key scopes are disabled. P2: The "missing API Key" test still sets `X-N8N-API-KEY`, so it never covers the no-header auth path. P2: The schema resolver never follows nested relative `$ref` files, so `include=schemas` returns partially unresolved request schemas. P2: Unsafe `as string` type cast on `req.headers['x-n8n-api-key']` — the value is `string | string[] | undefined`. If the header is an array (e.g. duplicated), the raw array would be passed into the DB query. Use a type guard instead. ```
Architecture diagram ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent as AI Agent / Client participant API as Discover Handler participant DB as ApiKeyRepository participant Service as Discover Service participant Spec as openapi.yml participant Middleware as Global Middleware participant Handlers as Handler Modules Note over Agent,Handlers: NEW: Capability Discovery Flow Agent->>API: GET /api/v1/discover (X-N8N-API-KEY) API->>DB: Fetch key & scopes alt API Key invalid DB-->>API: null API-->>Agent: 401 Unauthorized else API Key valid DB-->>API: { scopes: ["workflow:read", ...] } end API->>Service: buildDiscoverResponse(scopes, includeSchemas) opt If cache empty Service->>Spec: Load OpenAPI specification loop For each path/operation Service->>Handlers: Import operation handler file Handlers->>Middleware: NEW: Tag middleware with __apiKeyScope Service->>Service: NEW: Introspect middleware chain for scope tags Service->>Service: Map endpoint to resource tag & scope end Service->>Service: Cache parsed endpoint metadata end Service->>Service: Filter endpoints by caller's API key scopes opt include=schemas query param loop For each filtered endpoint Service->>Service: NEW: Recursively resolve $ref in request body Note right of Service: Handles nested/circular refs end end Service-->>API: DiscoverResponse (scoped resources) API-->>Agent: 200 OK (JSON capability map) Note over Service,Middleware: Middleware introspection allows zero-maintenance
discovery for new endpoints. ```
Reply with feedback, questions, or to request a fix. Tag `@cubic-dev-ai` to re-run a review.

body:
Not a scopesEnabled regression — community keys already have all role scopes stored (resolveScopesForUser gives getApiKeyScopesForRole when scopes aren't licensed), so the scope filter passes for sourceControl etc. regardless of the flag.

The real gap is that discover doesn't filter by license features (e.g. isLicensed middleware). Intentional for now — the actual endpoint returns 403 with a clear 'feature not licensed' message, which is self-explanatory. We can add license-aware filtering as a follow-up if it causes confusion.
author: shortstacked

body: Valid point — parse failures here are permanent (local YAML files). Removing the catch so a failed promise stays cached and isn't retried.

body:
I feel like lots of functions in this file around parsing the specs could be simplified a lot if we used `@apidevtools/json-schema-ref-parser`, which is already a shared dependency with `express-openapi-validator`. For instance, the `dereference` method already loads all the inner "refs" 

Also more generally speaking about how many times we load the specs file. index.ts already loads openapi.yml via yamljs for Swagger UI (with its own cache), and express-openapi-validator parses it again internally for request validation. The discover service would add  a third independent parse + cache (cachedEndpointsPromise).
author: guillaumejacquart

body: Brilliant, I didn't even realize we had the parser already that makes this way smaller, thanks!
author: shortstacked

body: That's fair, I don't think tokens need to be that short. And being clearer makes more sense.

<details>
<summary>Architecture diagram</summary>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Host as Host (Node.js)
    participant Eval as ExpressionEvaluator
    participant Isolate as V8 Isolate (Sandbox)
    participant Luxon as Luxon (Inside Isolate)
    participant Globals as globalThis / __data

    Note over Host,Globals: Expression Execution Initialization

    Host->>Eval: evaluate(expression, data, options)
    Eval->>Isolate: Execute resetDataProxies(timezone)
    
    Note over Isolate,Luxon: NEW: Internal DateTime Initialization
    
    Isolate->>Luxon: Set Settings.defaultZone = timezone
    
    Isolate->>Luxon: NEW: DateTime.now()
    Luxon-->>Isolate: Local DateTime Instance
    Isolate->>Globals: NEW: Assign to globalThis.$now
    
    Isolate->>Luxon: NEW: DateTime.now().set({h:0, m:0, s:0, ms:0})
    Luxon-->>Isolate: Local Date-only Instance
    Isolate->>Globals: NEW: Assign to globalThis.$today

    Note over Isolate,Globals: Data Proxy Setup (Existing)
    
    Isolate->>Globals: Create DeepLazyProxies ($json, $node, etc.)
    
    Note over Host,Globals: Runtime Expression Access

    Isolate->>Globals: Access {{ $now }} or {{ $today }}
    Globals-->>Isolate: Return local Luxon object (Prototype preserved)
    
    alt Property Access (e.g., $now.zoneName)
        Isolate->>Luxon: Invoke .zoneName
        Luxon-->>Isolate: Return timezone string
    end

    Isolate-->>Eval: Result of evaluation
    Eval-->>Host: Final value
```

body:
@ireneea I removed this `workflowRepository.update` call here, because I have found that it redundantly sets the versionId of the imported workflow to the `versionId` from the tracked workflow.json file in git.
Notifying you, since you added this update statement here: https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/commit/65942c3b7b1a643e415efcaba7b582d829623160#diff-9372037b7cadf07a667f84f65e8ace7bdb924915f484d2aebfe5249d68c036baR635

Why do I deem it redundant?
Because higher above inside the `importWorkflowFromWorkFolder` method, there is a [workflowRepository.upsert()](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/blob/c2757ae9e467c70a81ba14a40920c6983fbd432c/packages/cli/src/environments.ee/source-control/source-control-import.service.ee.ts#L693) call, that will already set the `versionId` of the imported workflow to the versionId from the json file.

Would be great if you can take a look as well and maybe add why the `workflowRepository.update({ id: existingWorkflow.id }, { versionId: importedWorkflow.versionId })` should still remain in addition to the earlier `upsert`.
author: konstantintieber

<sub>Reply `@AikidoSec ignore: [REASON]` to ignore this issue.</sub>

author: aikido-pr-checks

body:
**35 issues found** across 76 files

**Note:** This PR contains a large number of files. cubic only reviews up to 75 files per PR, so some files may not have been reviewed.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately.


<file name="scripts/n8n-password-hash.txt">

<violation number="1" location="scripts/n8n-password-hash.txt:1">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Security Review – Credential & Secret Handling: do not commit a reusable owner password hash. This file is wired into authentication, so the repository now contains a hardcoded login secret for seeded n8n instances.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker-compose.yml">

<violation number="1" location="docker-compose.yml:28">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Bind this port to localhost. `5678:5678` exposes n8n's login and first-run owner registration flow on all host interfaces, so another machine on the network can reach the instance and claim the owner account.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="docker-compose.yml:40">
P2: The compose file claims NSOLID_OTLP_CONFIG can be overridden via .env/env vars, but the environment block hard-codes a literal JSON string. Docker Compose only applies .env/shell values when referenced via `${NSOLID_OTLP_CONFIG}` or key-only entries, so the documented override will never take effect.</violation>
</file>

<file name="scripts/dockerize-n8n.mjs">

<violation number="1" location="scripts/dockerize-n8n.mjs:143">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Remove the hardcoded owner password hash. The new `--run` flow creates an owner with a known default credential, which ships a predictable privileged login in source.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="scripts/dockerize-n8n.mjs:286">
P2: `--run` now enables owner creation by default and falls back to hard-coded credentials unless users explicitly set N8N_CREATE_OWNER=false. This contradicts the script’s own usage contract and can start a locally published UI with predictable default admin credentials. Owner creation should be opt-in.</violation>
</file>

<file name="AGENTS.md">

<violation number="1" location="AGENTS.md:16">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Do not document or rely on a repository-committed default owner password hash. This creates a predictable admin credential path for Docker deployments; require `N8N_OWNER_HASH` to come from a runtime secret instead.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="AGENTS.md:61">
P2: Testing stack documentation is inaccurate: frontend tests use Vitest, but AGENTS.md now states unit tests are run with Jest only. This can mislead contributors into using the wrong runner and mocking APIs.</violation>

<violation number="3" location="AGENTS.md:104">
P3: AGENTS.md ends with a conversational prompt to the user, which can derail agent workflows; this file should contain durable instructions only.</violation>
</file>

<file name="scripts/hash-password.sh">

<violation number="1" location="scripts/hash-password.sh:20">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Do not take the plaintext n8n password from `argv`; it is exposed in shell history and process listings before hashing.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="scripts/hash-password.sh:43">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Do not interpolate the password into the `node -e` source; a crafted password can execute arbitrary JavaScript while generating the hash.</violation>

<violation number="3" location="scripts/hash-password.sh:45">
P2: Omitting `output-file` can never print the hash to stdout because `OUTFILE` is always defaulted to a path, so the file-write branch always executes.</violation>
</file>

<file name="README.md">

<violation number="1" location="README.md:89">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Remove the documented hardcoded default owner password. This build/run path auto-creates the initial n8n owner and falls back to a known bcrypt hash, which makes the admin account predictable to anyone who uses the default flow.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker/images/n8n/docker-entrypoint.sh">

<violation number="1" location="docker/images/n8n/docker-entrypoint.sh:9">
P2: Owner bootstrap is hard-wired to a SQLite file path and uses a SQLite-only helper, so N8N_CREATE_OWNER will always time out and exit 1 on Postgres/non-default DB paths. This breaks startup for non-SQLite deployments that enable owner creation.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="docker/images/n8n/docker-entrypoint.sh:38">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

**Hardcoded default credentials** — violates the Security Review rule: "Hardcoded secrets, API keys, or tokens in code."

The `update_owner_async` function falls back to a hardcoded email (`techyactor15@gmail.com`) and a bcrypt hash whose plaintext (`ExamplePassword123!`) is documented in a comment. If a user sets `N8N_CREATE_OWNER=true` but omits any of the `N8N_OWNER_*` env vars, the system silently creates an admin account with publicly known credentials.

These defaults should be removed. Instead, require all credential env vars when `N8N_CREATE_OWNER=true` and exit with a clear error if any are missing.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker/images/n8n/owner-bootstrap.js">

<violation number="1" location="docker/images/n8n/owner-bootstrap.js:75">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

**Hardcoded external email as default owner is a critical security risk.** The `updateOwner()` function defaults the owner account to `techyactor15@gmail.com` (an unknown personal Gmail) when `N8N_OWNER_EMAIL` is not set. If a deployer provides a password hash but forgets the email variable, the n8n instance owner is silently set to this address — effectively a backdoor. There should be no hardcoded fallback email; the script should fail if required environment variables are missing.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/cli/src/middlewares/cors.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/cli/src/middlewares/cors.ts:27">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Restrict the 172.x allowlist to the private 172.16.0.0/12 range. As written, this trusts public `172.*` origins and reflects them with `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true`, enabling authenticated cross-origin reads.</violation>
</file>

<file name="scripts/add-owner.sh">

<violation number="1" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:27">
P2: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Owner credentials (email, name) are hardcoded with no environment variable fallback, regressing from the `dockerize-n8n.mjs` flow that supported `N8N_OWNER_EMAIL`, `N8N_OWNER_FIRSTNAME`, and `N8N_OWNER_LASTNAME` overrides. Use env vars with these as defaults.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:31">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Hardcoded bcrypt password hash in source code. Per the security rule, secrets must not be hardcoded. Additionally, this hash differs from the canonical one in `scripts/n8n-password-hash.txt` that AGENTS.md specifies. Read the hash from that file or accept it via an environment variable (e.g. `N8N_OWNER_HASH`), matching the pattern the existing mjs scripts use.</violation>

<violation number="3" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:38">
P1: Raw copy/restore of only `database.sqlite` is unsafe for live/WAL SQLite volumes and can cause data loss or inconsistent DB state.</violation>

<violation number="4" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:49">
P2: Unescaped variable interpolation in SQL literals can break owner upsert when values contain apostrophes.</violation>

<violation number="5" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:53">
P2: Updating an existing owner overwrites the entire `settings` JSON, deleting other persisted user settings keys instead of merging `userActivated`.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker/images/n8n/tracing.js">

<violation number="1" location="docker/images/n8n/tracing.js:81">
P2: Tracing bootstrap ignores OTLP signal-specific endpoint env vars, forcing both exporters to use a single derived base URL.</violation>
</file>

<file name="LOGS/n8n-runtime-log-1773091388.log">

<violation number="1" location="LOGS/n8n-runtime-log-1773091388.log:1">
P2: A generated runtime log containing PII and environment details was committed to the repository.</violation>
</file>

<file name="Dockerfile">

<violation number="1" location="Dockerfile:67">
P2: OpenTelemetry packages are installed from incompatible release trains (experimental 0.57.x with stable 1.23.0/api 1.9.0), which conflicts with the official compatibility matrix and can cause peer-dependency conflicts or duplicate core packages.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="Dockerfile:113">
P2: The image sets an ENTRYPOINT but never overrides the base image’s default CMD. debian:bookworm-slim defaults to `CMD ["bash"]`, and your entrypoint forwards args directly to n8n, so `docker run n8n-nsolid` will pass `bash` to n8n instead of starting normally. Add an explicit CMD (or clear it) so the container starts n8n by default.</violation>
</file>

<file name="LOGS/dockerize-log-1773091118.log">

<violation number="1" location="LOGS/dockerize-log-1773091118.log:79">
P2: Committed generated Docker log exposes personal email and local environment metadata; remove it from version control.</violation>
</file>

<file name="SECURITY_VULNERABILITIES_MASTER.md">

<violation number="1" location="SECURITY_VULNERABILITIES_MASTER.md:5">
P2: Security report counts and alert listings are inconsistent (missing Dependabot IDs and mismatched totals), making the master document unreliable for tracking remediation.</violation>
</file>

<file name="LOGS/dockerize-log-1773091388.log">

<violation number="1" location="LOGS/dockerize-log-1773091388.log:80">
P2: A committed runtime log file exposes a personal email address and local environment details in Git history.</violation>
</file>

<file name=".github/workflows/codeql.yml">

<violation number="1" location=".github/workflows/codeql.yml:63">
P2: Workflow uses mutable action tags (e.g., @v4) instead of pinning to commit SHAs, which leaves the CI pipeline open to supply‑chain risks if tags are retargeted or compromised.</violation>
</file>

<file name=".codacy/cli.sh">

<violation number="1" location=".codacy/cli.sh:7">
P2: Codacy shim hardcodes a single Homebrew path, making the repo entrypoint fail when `codacy-cli` is installed elsewhere.</violation>
</file>

<file name="LOGS/n8n-runtime-log-1773091118.log">

<violation number="1" location="LOGS/n8n-runtime-log-1773091118.log:1">
P2: A committed runtime log file exposes a personal email and environment-specific runtime details in repository history.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker/images/n8n/Dockerfile">

<violation number="1" location="docker/images/n8n/Dockerfile:10">
P2: NODE_PATH interpolates ${NODE_VERSION} after FROM, but NODE_VERSION is never re-declared in this stage, so the expansion can be empty and point to a non-existent global modules directory.</violation>
</file>

<file name="SECURITY_FIXES_TRACKING.md">

<violation number="1" location="SECURITY_FIXES_TRACKING.md:5">
P2: Security tracking counts are internally inconsistent (header, summary, and detailed tables do not reconcile), reducing reliability of vulnerability remediation tracking.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="SECURITY_FIXES_TRACKING.md:269">
P2: CORS status is contradictory within the same tracking file (marked both FIXED and NOT FIXED), making remediation state unreliable.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker/images/n8n/package.json">

<violation number="1" location="docker/images/n8n/package.json:11">
P2: OpenTelemetry dependencies are pinned to mismatched release families (0.57.x experimental with 1.23.x stable). The official compatibility matrix pairs 0.57.x with 1.30.x, so this mix can install incompatible copies of resources/semconv/metrics when tracing.js uses them together.</violation>
</file>
```

</details>

**35 issues found** across 76 files

**Note:** This PR contains a large number of files. cubic only reviews up to 75 files per PR, so some files may not have been reviewed.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues)</summary>

```text

Check if these issues are valid — if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately.


<file name="scripts/n8n-password-hash.txt">

<violation number="1" location="scripts/n8n-password-hash.txt:1">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Security Review – Credential & Secret Handling: do not commit a reusable owner password hash. This file is wired into authentication, so the repository now contains a hardcoded login secret for seeded n8n instances.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker-compose.yml">

<violation number="1" location="docker-compose.yml:28">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Bind this port to localhost. `5678:5678` exposes n8n's login and first-run owner registration flow on all host interfaces, so another machine on the network can reach the instance and claim the owner account.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="docker-compose.yml:40">
P2: The compose file claims NSOLID_OTLP_CONFIG can be overridden via .env/env vars, but the environment block hard-codes a literal JSON string. Docker Compose only applies .env/shell values when referenced via `${NSOLID_OTLP_CONFIG}` or key-only entries, so the documented override will never take effect.</violation>
</file>

<file name="scripts/dockerize-n8n.mjs">

<violation number="1" location="scripts/dockerize-n8n.mjs:143">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Remove the hardcoded owner password hash. The new `--run` flow creates an owner with a known default credential, which ships a predictable privileged login in source.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="scripts/dockerize-n8n.mjs:286">
P2: `--run` now enables owner creation by default and falls back to hard-coded credentials unless users explicitly set N8N_CREATE_OWNER=false. This contradicts the script’s own usage contract and can start a locally published UI with predictable default admin credentials. Owner creation should be opt-in.</violation>
</file>

<file name="AGENTS.md">

<violation number="1" location="AGENTS.md:16">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Do not document or rely on a repository-committed default owner password hash. This creates a predictable admin credential path for Docker deployments; require `N8N_OWNER_HASH` to come from a runtime secret instead.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="AGENTS.md:61">
P2: Testing stack documentation is inaccurate: frontend tests use Vitest, but AGENTS.md now states unit tests are run with Jest only. This can mislead contributors into using the wrong runner and mocking APIs.</violation>

<violation number="3" location="AGENTS.md:104">
P3: AGENTS.md ends with a conversational prompt to the user, which can derail agent workflows; this file should contain durable instructions only.</violation>
</file>

<file name="scripts/hash-password.sh">

<violation number="1" location="scripts/hash-password.sh:20">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Do not take the plaintext n8n password from `argv`; it is exposed in shell history and process listings before hashing.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="scripts/hash-password.sh:43">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Do not interpolate the password into the `node -e` source; a crafted password can execute arbitrary JavaScript while generating the hash.</violation>

<violation number="3" location="scripts/hash-password.sh:45">
P2: Omitting `output-file` can never print the hash to stdout because `OUTFILE` is always defaulted to a path, so the file-write branch always executes.</violation>
</file>

<file name="README.md">

<violation number="1" location="README.md:89">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Remove the documented hardcoded default owner password. This build/run path auto-creates the initial n8n owner and falls back to a known bcrypt hash, which makes the admin account predictable to anyone who uses the default flow.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker/images/n8n/docker-entrypoint.sh">

<violation number="1" location="docker/images/n8n/docker-entrypoint.sh:9">
P2: Owner bootstrap is hard-wired to a SQLite file path and uses a SQLite-only helper, so N8N_CREATE_OWNER will always time out and exit 1 on Postgres/non-default DB paths. This breaks startup for non-SQLite deployments that enable owner creation.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="docker/images/n8n/docker-entrypoint.sh:38">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

**Hardcoded default credentials** — violates the Security Review rule: "Hardcoded secrets, API keys, or tokens in code."

The `update_owner_async` function falls back to a hardcoded email (`techyactor15@gmail.com`) and a bcrypt hash whose plaintext (`ExamplePassword123!`) is documented in a comment. If a user sets `N8N_CREATE_OWNER=true` but omits any of the `N8N_OWNER_*` env vars, the system silently creates an admin account with publicly known credentials.

These defaults should be removed. Instead, require all credential env vars when `N8N_CREATE_OWNER=true` and exit with a clear error if any are missing.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker/images/n8n/owner-bootstrap.js">

<violation number="1" location="docker/images/n8n/owner-bootstrap.js:75">
P0: Custom agent: **Security Review**

**Hardcoded external email as default owner is a critical security risk.** The `updateOwner()` function defaults the owner account to `techyactor15@gmail.com` (an unknown personal Gmail) when `N8N_OWNER_EMAIL` is not set. If a deployer provides a password hash but forgets the email variable, the n8n instance owner is silently set to this address — effectively a backdoor. There should be no hardcoded fallback email; the script should fail if required environment variables are missing.</violation>
</file>

<file name="packages/cli/src/middlewares/cors.ts">

<violation number="1" location="packages/cli/src/middlewares/cors.ts:27">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Restrict the 172.x allowlist to the private 172.16.0.0/12 range. As written, this trusts public `172.*` origins and reflects them with `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true`, enabling authenticated cross-origin reads.</violation>
</file>

<file name="scripts/add-owner.sh">

<violation number="1" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:27">
P2: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Owner credentials (email, name) are hardcoded with no environment variable fallback, regressing from the `dockerize-n8n.mjs` flow that supported `N8N_OWNER_EMAIL`, `N8N_OWNER_FIRSTNAME`, and `N8N_OWNER_LASTNAME` overrides. Use env vars with these as defaults.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:31">
P1: Custom agent: **Security Review**

Hardcoded bcrypt password hash in source code. Per the security rule, secrets must not be hardcoded. Additionally, this hash differs from the canonical one in `scripts/n8n-password-hash.txt` that AGENTS.md specifies. Read the hash from that file or accept it via an environment variable (e.g. `N8N_OWNER_HASH`), matching the pattern the existing mjs scripts use.</violation>

<violation number="3" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:38">
P1: Raw copy/restore of only `database.sqlite` is unsafe for live/WAL SQLite volumes and can cause data loss or inconsistent DB state.</violation>

<violation number="4" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:49">
P2: Unescaped variable interpolation in SQL literals can break owner upsert when values contain apostrophes.</violation>

<violation number="5" location="scripts/add-owner.sh:53">
P2: Updating an existing owner overwrites the entire `settings` JSON, deleting other persisted user settings keys instead of merging `userActivated`.</violation>
</file>

<file name="docker/images/n8n/tracing.js">

<violation number="1" location="docker/images/n8n/tracing.js:81">
P2: Tracing bootstrap ignores OTLP signal-specific endpoint env vars, forcing both exporters to use a single derived base URL.</violation>
</file>

<file name="LOGS/n8n-runtime-log-1773091388.log">

<violation number="1" location="LOGS/n8n-runtime-log-1773091388.log:1">
P2: A generated runtime log containing PII and environment details was committed to the repository.</violation>

<file name="Dockerfile">

<violation number="1" location="Dockerfile:67">
P2: OpenTelemetry packages are installed from incompatible release trains (experimental 0.57.x with stable 1.23.0/api 1.9.0), which conflicts with the official compatibility matrix and can cause peer-dependency conflicts or duplicate core packages.</violation>

<violation number="2" location="Dockerfile:113">
P2: The image sets an ENTRYPOINT but never overrides the base image’s default CMD. debian:bookworm-slim defaults to `CMD ["bash"]`, and your entrypoint forwards args directly to n8n, so `docker run n8n-nsolid` will pass `bash` to n8n instead of starting normally. Add an explicit CMD (or clear it) so the container starts n8n by default.</violation>
</file>

<file name="LOGS/dockerize-log-1773091118.log">

<violation number="1" location="LOGS/dockerize-log-1773091118.log:79">

<violation number="1" location="LOGS/dockerize-log-1773091388.log:80">
```

body:
P2: The image sets an ENTRYPOINT but never overrides the base image’s default CMD. debian:bookworm-slim defaults to `CMD ["bash"]`, and your entrypoint forwards args directly to n8n, so `docker run n8n-nsolid` will pass `bash` to n8n instead of starting normally. Add an explicit CMD (or clear it) so the container starts n8n by default.
Prompt for AI agents ```text Check if this issue is valid — if so, understand the root cause and fix it. At Dockerfile, line 113: The image sets an ENTRYPOINT but never overrides the base image’s default CMD. debian:bookworm-slim defaults to `CMD ["bash"]`, and your entrypoint forwards args directly to n8n, so `docker run n8n-nsolid` will pass `bash` to n8n instead of starting normally. Add an explicit CMD (or clear it) so the container starts n8n by default. @@ -0,0 +1,113 @@ +RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint.sh /add-owner.sh +USER node + +ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--", "/docker-entrypoint.sh"] ```

Please align the PR title/description with the actual scope (or split into focused PRs) to reduce review/merge risk.

body: The entrypoint hardcodes the n8n binary location under `/usr/lib/node_modules/n8n/bin/n8n`. Other tooling in this repo (e.g. the Playwright test image script) assumes `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/n8n`, and npm global installs commonly land under `/usr/local`. To avoid runtime breakage across images/environments, prefer invoking `n8n` via `command -v n8n` (or a single env var for the install root) rather than hardcoding the module path.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The origin allowlist is broader than the comment suggests: `hostname.startsWith('172.')` allows *all* 172.x.x.x hostnames, including public ranges outside 172.16.0.0/12. This weakens the intended restriction for the dev-only CORS middleware. Consider validating exact RFC1918 ranges (172.16/12, 10/8, 192.168/16) using an IP parser (and handle hostnames vs IPs explicitly).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `removeTags()` uses DOMPurify but instantiates it via `createDOMPurify()` without providing a DOM/window. `@n8n/expression-runtime` is intended to run in isolated/server contexts, so this is likely to throw at runtime or behave inconsistently across CJS/ESM builds. Consider either keeping a non-DOM implementation (e.g. tag-stripping logic) or wiring DOMPurify correctly for Node (e.g. via JSDOM/isomorphic wrapper) with a single shared instance rather than per-call creation.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This changes the deterministic webhook `secret` used for Webex subscriptions from MD5 to PBKDF2. Because Webex uses this secret to sign requests and the node validates signatures against `webhookData.secret`, existing webhooks created before this change will start failing signature verification until they are recreated. If this is intentional, it needs a migration/compatibility strategy (e.g. accept both secrets during a transition, or version the secret derivation and store which one was used when creating the webhook).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `hash-password.sh` interpolates the raw password into a JavaScript string inside `node -e` (single-quoted within a double-quoted shell string). Passwords containing `'`, backslashes, or newlines can break the script or lead to code injection. Pass the password via an environment variable or base64/stdin and have Node read it safely instead of string interpolation.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This script hardcodes owner PII and a bcrypt password hash in-repo. Even though it's a hash, it functions as a credential for anyone who gets access to the DB/instance and encourages accidental reuse across environments. Please remove hardcoded credentials and require them via env vars or interactive prompts, and avoid committing any real hashes/emails to the repository.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This file commits a bcrypt password hash to the repository. Even if it's not plaintext, it is credential material and can be used to authenticate if deployed. It should not be tracked in git; generate it locally and keep it out of the repo (e.g. via .gitignore) or store only a documented placeholder value.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The CodeQL workflow uses floating action tags (e.g. `actions/checkout@v4`, `github/codeql-action/*@v4`). This repo previously pinned actions to full commit SHAs, which is important for supply-chain security. Consider pinning these actions to specific SHAs as well.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This Dockerfile copies the shared `docker-entrypoint.sh`, which conditionally calls `/owner-bootstrap.js` when `N8N_CREATE_OWNER=true`, but this image does not copy `owner-bootstrap.js` into the filesystem. If the flag is enabled at runtime, the container will fail during bootstrap. Either include `owner-bootstrap.js` in this image too, or gate the owner-creation logic in the entrypoint on the file existing.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Allowing any `*.local` origin makes the allowlist depend on DNS rather than network locality, so a non-local origin could be allowed if it uses a `.local` hostname. If the intent is to support dev-only hostnames, prefer an explicit allowlist/configured list (or only allow `.local` when the hostname resolves to a private IP).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Defaulting owner creation credentials (email/name/hash) in-repo is a security risk and can lead to accidental deployments with a known password hash. Please remove the hardcoded defaults and require these values to be supplied explicitly via environment variables (or fail fast when `N8N_CREATE_OWNER=true` and required vars are missing).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This entrypoint hardcodes a default bcrypt hash and PII (email/name). Shipping a default password hash in the image makes it easy to accidentally deploy an instance with a known credential. Consider requiring `N8N_OWNER_HASH` (and other owner fields) to be set explicitly when `N8N_CREATE_OWNER=true`, and avoid embedding personal data in defaults.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The Dockerfile relocates the compiled n8n install to `/usr/lib/node_modules/n8n`, but there is existing repo tooling that patches `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/n8n` inside images (e.g. `packages/testing/playwright/scripts/build-test-image.mjs`). If those scripts are still used, this path change will break them. Consider keeping the canonical install location aligned with existing tooling (or updating all related scripts in the same PR).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

author: r00gm

body:
AFAIK

No, TypeORM doesn’t offer a way to avoid string interpolation for `WHERE IN (subquery)`. The getQuery() + setParameters() pattern is the standard approach. It’s also what `execution.repository.ts `uses. The interpolated string is safe because `getQuery()` returns generated SQL (not user input), and all user values go through parameterized `setParameters()`.

author: Joffcom

body: ## Pull request overview This PR aims to fix the Oracle Database node crashing with `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded` when appending very large result sets, by replacing `push.apply` / spread-based appends with `concat()`. **Changes:** - Replaced spread-based appends in `_getResponseForOutbinds` with `concat()`. - Replaced `push.apply` usage in `configureQueryRunner` (transaction + independently modes) with `concat()` for accumulating `returnData`. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
`_getResponseForOutbinds` no longer mutates the `returnData` array passed in. `concat()` returns a new array, and reassigning the local parameter doesn’t update the caller’s array reference, so out bind results won’t be returned (call sites pass in `returnData`/`resultOutBinds` expecting it to be filled). Use an in-place append strategy (e.g., loop pushing items) or change this helper to return the new array and update all callers accordingly.
```suggestion
				returnData.push(...executionData);
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Building `returnData` via repeated `returnData = returnData.concat(executionData)` creates a new array and copies the accumulated results on every iteration. For large result sets and/or many queries this can become O(n²) time/memory and may negate the intent of the fix. Prefer appending in-place (e.g., iterate over `executionData` and `push` each item, or chunked appends) to keep overall complexity linear.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Same concern here: `returnData = returnData.concat(...)` copies the entire accumulated array each time. With multiple queries and large datasets this can cause substantial CPU/GC overhead. Use an in-place append approach to avoid repeated full-array copies.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The change from spread/apply to `concat()` is intended to handle large result sets safely, but there’s no unit test covering the large-array append path (or the out bind aggregation path) in this file. Adding a test that simulates a very large `executionData` / `outBinds` array and asserts the full output is returned would help prevent regressions like broken out bind handling or performance-safe accumulation changes.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

<summary>Architecture diagram</summary>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant App as Application Code
    participant Loader as RecursiveUrlLoader (@langchain/community)
    participant Validator as validateSafeUrl()
    participant HTTP as fetch()
    participant External as External Web Server
    participant Internal as Internal Service (e.g. Metadata API)

    Note over Loader,Internal: NEW: Secure Redirect Validation Flow (CVE-2026-27795)

    App->>Loader: load(targetUrl)
    
    Loader->>Validator: Check initial targetUrl
    Validator-->>Loader: Valid (Public IP)

    loop Redirect Handling (Max Limit)
        Loader->>HTTP: CHANGED: fetch(url) with { redirect: 'manual' }
        HTTP->>External: GET Request
        External-->>HTTP: 302 Redirect (Location: maliciousUrl)
        HTTP-->>Loader: Response (302)

        Loader->>Loader: NEW: Extract Location header
        
        alt SSRF Attack (Redirect to internal/metadata)
            Loader->>Validator: NEW: validateSafeUrl(maliciousUrl)
            Note right of Validator: Checks for 169.254.x.x, 10.x.x.x, etc.
            Validator-->>Loader: REJECTED (Unsafe)
            Loader-->>App: Error: SSRF Redirect Blocked
        else Legitimate Redirect
            Loader->>Validator: NEW: validateSafeUrl(safeRedirectUrl)
            Validator-->>Loader: OK
            Loader->>HTTP: fetch(safeRedirectUrl, { redirect: 'manual' })
        end
    end

    opt Safe Response (200 OK)
        HTTP->>External: GET Request
        External-->>HTTP: Content (HTML/Text)
        HTTP-->>Loader: Response Data
        Loader-->>App: Document[]
    end
```
</details>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant App as Application Code
    participant Undici as Undici Dispatcher
    participant Parser as HTTP/WS Parser (llhttp)
    participant Interceptor as Interceptors (Decompress/Dedupe)
    participant Server as External Server (Malicious/Standard)

    Note over App,Server: Security-Hardened Request/Response Flow (v6.24.0)

    App->>Undici: request({ upgrade, headers })
    
    rect rgb(23, 37, 84)
        Note right of Undici: NEW: Security Validation Layer
        Undici->>Undici: NEW: Validate 'upgrade' for CRLF (\r\n)
        Undici->>Undici: CHANGED: Deduplicate Content-Length headers (case-insensitive)
    end

    Undici->>Server: HTTP/1.1 Request (Sanitized)
    Server-->>Undici: HTTP Response (Chained Encodings / Large Body)

    alt Response Decompression
        Undici->>Interceptor: Handle Content-Encoding (gzip, br)
        rect rgb(23, 37, 84)
            Interceptor->>Interceptor: NEW: Limit decompression chain length (RFC 9110)
        end
        Interceptor-->>Undici: Decompressed Stream
    else Request Deduplication Enabled
        Undici->>Interceptor: deduplicate()
        rect rgb(23, 37, 84)
            Interceptor->>App: CHANGED: Stream chunks immediately (no full-body accumulation)
        end
    end

    Note over Undici,Server: WebSocket Interaction Flow

    Server->>Parser: 64-bit Length Frame / Compressed Frame
    
    rect rgb(23, 37, 84)
        alt Frame Header Parsing
            Parser->>Parser: NEW: Validate 64-bit length against overflow
        else Decompression (permessage-deflate)
            Parser->>Parser: NEW: Validate server_max_window_bits (Range 8-15)
            Parser->>Parser: NEW: Enforce memory limits on decompressed output
        end
    end

    alt Invalid Input Detected
        Parser-->>Undici: Throw Error
        Undici-->>App: Emit 'error' or 'close' (Prevent Process Crash)
    else Valid Data
        Parser-->>App: Emit 'message' / Data
    end
```
</details>

body:
Pushed 8fdf6c0e:

1. Replaced the `isContainedWithin`-as-router heuristic with explicit `isLegacyAbsolute` detection (`isWindowsFilePath || startsWith('/')`) in `load-nodes-and-credentials`, matching your suggestion.

2. Normalized backslashes from `path.relative()` before embedding in URL strings in `directory-loader.ts` (Qodo also flagged this), so Windows-emitted backslashes don't leak into HTTP URL paths.

3. Added regression coverage for Windows-style separators in `directory-loader.test`.

Local `pnpm install` hit a registry timeout in this environment, so I haven't run the test locally; CI will validate. Happy to fix-forward if it surfaces anything.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Redact command args before propagating npm failures**

This now throws `UnexpectedError(errorMessage, ...)` directly, where `errorMessage` includes the full command string built from `args.join(' ')`; in install paths `executeNpmCommand` is called with registry arguments (for example `--registry=<url>`), and the controller forwards `error.message` to API responses/events. If a custom registry URL contains embedded credentials, a failed npm command will leak those secrets to users/logs. Keep verbose details in logs or `cause`, but return a sanitized message from this throw path.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
author: chatgpt-codex-connector

<details>
<summary>Architecture diagram</summary>

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant S as CommunityPackageService
    participant U as npm-utils.ts
    participant R as NPM Registry (External API)
    participant CS as NEW: cross-spawn
    participant OS as Operating System (Win/Unix)
    participant N as npm CLI (npm / npm.cmd)

    Note over S,N: Package Integrity Verification Flow
    
    S->>U: verifyIntegrity(packageName)
    U->>R: HTTP GET (dist.integrity)
    
    alt HTTP Success
        R-->>U: Return checksum
        U-->>S: Validated
    else HTTP Failure (Fallback Triggered)
        Note right of U: CHANGED: Improved fallback execution
        U->>CS: executeNpmCommand(['view', 'dist.integrity'])
        
        CS->>OS: CHANGED: Resolve command path
        Note right of OS: On Windows, resolves "npm" to "npm.cmd"
        
        OS->>N: Spawn process
        
        N-->>CS: Stream stdout/stderr
        
        alt Process Success (Exit 0)
            CS-->>U: Return stdout
            U-->>S: Return integrity string
        else CHANGED: Process Failure (Exit != 0 or ENOENT)
            N-->>CS: Capture stderr data & exit code
            CS-->>U: Throw Error with full context (stderr + code)
            
            alt NEW: Error Mapping
                U->>U: Regex match stderr (404, ENOSPC, etc.)
                U-->>S: Throw UnexpectedError(Specific Message)
            else Unrecognized Error
                U-->>S: NEW: Throw Error(Command failed: npm... + stderr)
                Note over S: Replaces incorrect "Checksum failed" error
            end
        end
    end
```

body: This runs on an interval so there can be two different instance of this function execution at the same time. It would much easier to reason about this if we didn't allow concurrent executions of this function
